audio_clips.text: LUNAR ECLIPSE

The scene opens with a bright sun on the left side of the screen, casting radiant beams across space. The Earth is positioned in the middle-right, and the moon is shown further to the right, entering the Earth's shadow. As the video progresses, yellow lines appear, illustrating the path of sunlight being blocked by the Earth, leading to the darkening of the moon.

transcript.text: This phenomenon allows us to see the moon in a reddish color, almost as if it were bleeding.
Lunar eclipses are quite frequent and can be seen from any part of the world at night time.

audio_clips.text: A rocket stands on a launch pad at night, with a large reddish moon partially obscured behind it. The scene is illuminated by bright floodlights from surrounding towers.
The camera zooms in slightly, focusing more closely on the rocket and the moon, which now appears larger and more vividly colored as it rises higher into the sky.
The moon continues to rise, becoming fully visible above the rocket. It glows brightly against the dark night sky, casting an orange hue over the scene.

audio_clips.text: SOLAR ECLIPSE

The scene opens with a bright depiction of the Sun, Earth, and Moon in space. The Sun is shown on the left side emitting radiant beams of light. The Earth and Moon are positioned to the right, with the Moon between the Sun and Earth, illustrating the alignment during a solar eclipse.
Yellow lines appear emanating from the Sun, crossing through the Moon and casting a shadow onto the Earth, visually demonstrating how the Moon blocks the Sun's light during an eclipse.
The camera slowly zooms in on the alignment of the Sun, Moon, and Earth, emphasizing the shadow cast by the Moon onto the Earth, highlighting the mechanics of a solar eclipse.
